regime_justification: USDJPY holding near 159.38, BOJ meeting April 27-28 widely priced for hold (not hike), JGB 10Y at 2.44% climbing, MOF verbal intervention escalating, vol subdued despite event risk—classic pre-intervention watch conditions without active unwind.

domino_stage.label: Pressure Building
domino_stage.justification: USDJPY at 159.38 is 42 pips below 160 watch-line, pace moderate (+0.2% 5d by calc), JGB yields rising on Iran energy shock (¥/bbl = 15,113), BOJ rhetoric shifting to pause-then-June-hike, MOF Katayama verbal intervention intensifying—yen pressure evident but no trigger yet.

1w.label: Watchful
1w.driver: BOJ April 27-28 meeting priced for hold, USDJPY 42 pips from 160, intervention risk elevated but not imminent; applied unwind prior (data ambiguous, would otherwise grade D)

2w.label: Watchful
2w.driver: Post-BOJ window: if hold confirmed and USDJPY pushes above 160 on disappointment, MOF action risk spikes; US-Iran talks fragile, oil vol elevated

3w.label: Unlikely
3w.driver: If BOJ signals June normalization and oil eases on diplomacy, carry stabilizes; scenario-weighted baseline assumes ceasefire extension holds

4w.label: Unlikely
4w.driver: Fed hold May 7, BOJ June meeting still 6 weeks out; absent USDJPY acceleration or Hormuz closure, carry regime likely persists into mid-May

bottom_line: Carry regime yellow: USDJPY 42 pips below 160, BOJ pausing April 27-28, JGB climbing on energy shock, MOF jawboning intensifying. Vol subdued (7.7 1M IV) despite twin policy risk. Grades C/C/D/D: watchful through BOJ meeting window, then easing if June normalization signaled. Trigger map shows MOF intervention and BOJ surprise both armed-to-cocked; energy/basis latent. Crypto steady (BTC ~$77.6k) tracking risk-on. Operator takeaway: setup fragile but no ignition yet—monitor BOJ tone and USDJPY pace post-meeting.

trigger_map.trigger: Energy / Hormuz shock pushing ¥/bbl > prior peak

No current USD funding stress signals; 3M FX swap basis data unavailable but no headlines suggest dislocation

narrative.p1: USDJPY closed 159.38 Friday, down 0.13% on day but stable on week, holding 42 pips below the 160 MOF watch-line. Overnight news flow centered on fragile US-Iran diplomacy: reports suggested Iranian Foreign Minister Araghchi expected in Islamabad for talks, but Iran state media called them 'waste of time.' WTI pulled back to $94.88 (-1% Friday) but is +14% weekly on Hormuz blockade; ceasefire extended indefinitely per Trump. Japan core CPI came in at 1.8% (March), first acceleration in 5 months but still below BOJ's 2% target, removing urgency for an April hike. JGB 10Y climbed to 2.44%, a 1-week high, driven by energy-shock inflation expectations. MOF Finance Minister Katayama escalated verbal intervention, telling G7 she's watching FX 'with a high sense of urgency' and coordinating with US Treasury's Bessent. Nikkei 225 hit record high 59,716 (+0.97%) on tech strength; VIX fell 3.11% to 18.71 as S&P/Nasdaq printed all-time highs. Regime remains yellow; no stage change from yesterday's 1 (Pressure Building).
narrative.p2: The quant anchors paint a fragile but not yet ignited picture. US-JP 10Y spread at 187 bps is still wide, supporting the carry trade; carry-to-vol proxy is ~24 (187 / 7.72), historically comfortable. But the yen's underperformance is now domestically driven: USDJPY has risen while US-Japan yield differentials narrowed, breaking the classic carry correlation (per MUFG and forex.com analysis). Distance from 60d mean is neutral, and 5-day pace is moderate (~+0.2% by my calc from 159.59 to 159.38 is actually flat to slight down—FX spot moved sideways this week). The key risk is post-BOJ disappointment: market prices only 7% hike probability for April 27-28, expecting a hold with guidance shift toward June. If BOJ delivers a bland hold, USDJPY could test 160+ on dovish repricing, triggering MOF action. The yen/bbl anchor (15,113) is elevated, adding imported inflation pressure that keeps BOJ normalization on the table for June. JGB 10Y at 2.44% is climbing but not spiking; MOVE at 67 shows rates vol is asleep. VIX at 18.71 and equity all-time highs confirm the market is leaning risk-on, funded by yen carry. Applied unwind prior to 1-week grade: data is ambiguous (USDJPY stable, vol low, carry spread wide, but MOF jawboning + BOJ meeting = binary event risk), so tiebreaker nudges D→C. Without the prior, I'd grade 1w D (unlikely) given benign spot price action; with it, C (watchful) reflects the fragile setup into the BOJ meeting.
narrative.p3: BTC at $77,632 is consolidating after a 4-week rally; down 0.80% on day but +1.6% on week per CoinGecko. Open interest data unavailable but crypto remains in sympathy with equity risk-on: S&P/Nasdaq highs and VIX sub-19 are classic carry-friendly conditions. BTC's resilience despite Japan-stress headlines suggests the market views yen carry risk as contained—no preemptive deleveraging yet. XRP and altcoins tracking BTC's sideways grind. For the operator: crypto liquidity is steady, not draining. If BOJ disappoints dovishly and USDJPY spikes above 160, watch for a brief vol pop and potential BTC dip as carry unwind fears flare; but absent a true MOF intervention or BOJ hawkish surprise, BTC likely consolidates in the $75k-$80k range, waiting for the next macro catalyst (Fed May 7, BOJ June). The setup supports a 'hold and watch' posture; no urgency to rotate out of risk unless USDJPY pace accelerates sharply or oil re-spikes above $100.

horizon_rationale.w1_w2: Grade unchanged C→C. Week 1 is dominated by BOJ April 27-28 meeting risk; market expects hold but any language shift matters. If BOJ holds without signaling June hike, USDJPY likely tests 160+, elevating MOF intervention risk (cocked→firing). If BOJ surprises hawkish or clearly flags June, yen strengthens and unwind risk eases. Week 2 covers post-BOJ price discovery and potential MOF response if USDJPY breaches 160 with pace. Both weeks remain 'watchful' because the binary event (BOJ) sits inside the window and outcome determines whether pressure dissipates or escalates.
horizon_rationale.w2_w3: Grade steps down C→D. By week 3 (mid-May), BOJ meeting will be in the rearview and market will be pricing June (June 15-16 meeting per BOJ calendar). If April meeting delivered a June normalization signal, carry unwind risk recedes as yen finds support on forward guidance. If BOJ was fully dovish and MOF intervened, week 3 is post-intervention stabilization (historically interventions are short-lived without policy support, but they break one-way momentum). US-Iran ceasefire extension holding suggests oil may ease from $95 toward $90 if Islamabad talks yield progress, reducing yen import-cost pressure. Scenario-weighted baseline: pressure easing, hence D.
horizon_rationale.w3_w4: Grade flat D→D. Week 4 takes us to late May; Fed holds May 7, BOJ June meeting is still 3 weeks away (early June). Absent a Hormuz closure or USDJPY acceleration above 162, carry regime likely persists. The 4-week grade reflects that the next major catalyst (BOJ June hike probability) is beyond the horizon, so unwind risk remains 'unlikely' in the May second-half window. If BOJ April meeting was dovish and USDJPY is range-bound 158-161 with MOF jawboning containing upside, the setup is stable but fragile—classic Stage 1. Non-monotonic path (C/C/D/D) reflects the binary BOJ event risk in weeks 1-2, then easing into late May as that risk resolves.

watch_list: BOJ April 27-28 statement language on June outlook and energy/inflation assessment
USDJPY pace in 24-48 hours post-BOJ: breach of 160 with >0.5% daily move would trigger MOF action
US-Iran Islamabad talks: any concrete breakthrough eases oil, yen import pressure, and reduces BOJ hike urgency
MOF intervention evidence: sudden USDJPY vol spike or unusual trading volume (monthly intervention data lags, watch real-time price action)
JGB 10Y: sustained break above 2.50% would force BOJ to address yield curve control legacy and accelerate normalization timeline
